entry.title: Re: SAP Community AI Conversation Chatbot to ask Q&A?
entry.content:
Hi @YashikaYTD - thank you for request. 

You're right that SAP Community is a vast knowledge repository with great potential for AI to make that content more accessible. While we’re actively exploring how to bring AI-powered experiences into the community, we’re not able to expose the data in such way as security, content governance, and quality controls are of utmost importance.

That said, we're excited about what’s possible and are taking thoughtful steps to shape the right approach.

I will add your idea/request to our new feedback list.

entry.title: Re: Adapt UI Not Available to Hide 'Confirm All' Button i...
entry.content:
In the Starter Customizing Environment, it is not possible to disable or hide UI elements like the "Confirm All" button in the Manage Service Orders app (F3571A). This is because Adapt UI and other key user extensibility features are not supported in starter systems—they are demo systems meant for fit-to-standard exploration only.

If this functionality is required, you can perform it in a test or development tenant, where Adapt UI is available and enabled through the relevant business catalogs (SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FLX_PC or SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FLX_CUS_PC).

For further reference, see:

KBA 3096740 – “Adapt UI” button is missing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

KBA 2981323 – Extensibility for Service: Missing “Adapt UI” option

entry.title: Re: Version Change in report writer report
entry.content:
You cannot (and must not) change the Program Version of Report Groups (tables T803V and T803VP), it's automatically incremented during an import, as explained below.

The Program Version is automatically incremented when these two conditions exist:

  1. An Extract (SAP Library link: Create Extract) exists for the current version (table GRIX).
  2. The report group is regenerated. It may happen if done manually (GR52) or during the import of the report group in the system.

The transport of a report group is indicated by this entry: R3TR TDAT GRW_JOB. It contains a table key T803J <client CHAR 3><report group CHAR 4> indicating the concerned report group. The post-import function module is G_RW_AFTER_IMPORT which calls CL_GRW_TRANSPORT_UTIL. For a report group, it calls the method AFTER_IMPORT of the class CL_GRW_TRANSPORT_JOB, which calls the function module G_RW_PROGRAM_ACTIVE_VERSION.

The generation of a report group also calls G_RW_PROGRAM_ACTIVE_VERSION.

G_RW_PROGRAM_ACTIVE_VERSION increments the program version if an Extract exists for the current program version; there is this comment in the code:

Active version must be always increased if extracts exist due to possible problems with dynamical sets (TVAL pointers in table COLUMNS)

entry.title: Real Estate Management with SAP​ and Planon
entry.content:
Better together: How SAP Expands its Cloud ERP Portfolio through partnering​

Business partnerships are extensions to Cloud ERP processes that are key for companies of all sizes to increase speed of innovation and to become more resilient. Teaming up with our partners not only unlocks innovation but ultimately provides faster time-to-value for our customers and addresses critical capabilities as part of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud road map. The way to embed partners and collaborate with them has changed over time: today, many SAP partners leverage the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to create extensions to Cloud ERP processes and experiences for our customers.​

Combining the strengths of market-leading organizations like Planon and Schneider Electric with the expertise of SAP will provide significant business value and innovation to customers’ real estate business by holistically connecting buildings, people, and processes. The cloud ERP solution from SAP serves as the backbone for managing end-to-end business processes, together with Planon, the market leader for vertical real estate and facility management software, and Schneider Electric, a global specialist in energy management and automation for buildings.​

Customers will benefit from the data flowing from smart technologies seamlessly into ERP processes. This will unleash the potential of the huge amount of real estate-related data, creating new opportunities for optimization and value creation.​

The partnership with Planon follows a strategically aligned partnership approach which includes: ​

  • Strong product co-innovation and collaboration on roadmap and technology alignment to create integrated scenarios.​
  • Proven premium certification, where SAP’s validation increases customer confidence by providing the assurance of a fully tested, out-of-the-box, SAP-approved integration that meets all SAP product standards.​
  • Strong go-to-market collaboration with active co-selling, joint positioning, co-marketing, and executive sponsorship to bring the co-innovation to our customers.

Way forward ​

Planon Real Estate Management for SAP will have unique properties, with a jointly developed “out-of-the-box” integration with SAP S/4HANA as the most prominent one.​

The solution integration is based on a division of capabilities between SAP andPlanon. The key capabilities of both solutions are shared as following:​

Real Estate and Facility Management processes are supported in Planon, while related Finance processes are supported in SAP S/4HANA.

 

​

Planon and SAP are using an aligned data model based on the SAP One Domain Model (ODM). This enables ONE single point of truth for master data management.​

Real Estate Data includes:​

  • Architecture Objects​
  • Usable Objects (Accounting Responsibility)​
  • Rentable Objects​
  • Contracts​

Additional Master Data objects like Business Partners, Accounting Objects or Fixed Assets are also supported.​ 

The interaction of this joined data is shown in the following picture.​

Planon and SAP run the relevant processes on this joined data foundation and ensure a transactional process flow between both applications.​

The transaction integration is done via “jump offs” (Deep Links and Intent based Navigation),dedicated interfaces (FIBTI-Finance Business Transaction Interface) or integration flows of the SAP Cloud Integration Suite. 

Demo video​

The following video shows the interaction between Planon and SAP S/4HANA using a shopping mall as an example. The steps shown are:​

  • SAP Fiori launchpad in SAP S/4HANA and jump to Planon​
  • Planon Homepage with overviews​
  • Detailed view of the Paris Shopping Mall​
  • Occupancy and vacancy overview in 2D CAD drawing​
  • Detailed view of a rentable unit and direct jump off to SAP S/4HANA​
  • Lease-Out Contract in SAP S/4HANA​
  • Return to Planon to the detail view of the rentable unit​
  • Jump to rental contract in SAP S/4HANA for the cost overview of the shopping mall​
  • Review Booklet with Plan/Actuals details​

Link to demo video: https://dam.sap.com/mac/download/ap/j95i3te?rc=10&doi=SAP1207364 

How to get ​

Planon Real Estate Management for SAP S/4HANA is an SAP Endorsed App and available on the SAP Store. An SAP Endorsed App is a third-party solution that has been certified and specially approved by SAP for its high quality, performance, and ability to meet specific customer needs within the SAP ecosystem. These apps go beyond standard SAP certification and are considered “top-tier” solutions in the SAP Store.​

As a third-party partner solution, it is sold by Planon. Planon is also responsible for the maintenance and operation of the solution.​

Deployment and Releases​

The integration between SAP and Planon is available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAPS/4HANA.​

Minimum Releases are:​

  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud: 2402​
  • SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud: 2023, FPS 1​
  • SAP S/4HANA On Premise: 2023, FPS 1​

Please be aware, that for new features a higher release might be required (see SAP help for more details).

Update for SAP Flexible Real Estate Management (RE-FX) ​

The functions of the component SAP RE-FX (Flexible Real Estate Management) are divided into two areas:​

  • SAP Contract and Lease Management: ​

This capability mainly includes contract management, contract accounting, condition adjustment, sales-based rent, contract valuation, and contract-specific standard reporting. ​

In addition, SAP Contract and Lease Management also includes the new functions developed for the integration with Planon, such as Usable Object, Rentable Object, Cost Settlement and Cost Allocation. These features are available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA .​

  • RE-FX functions for Real Estate Management: ​

This capability comprises the "old" real estate master data of the architectural and usage view (business entity, and so on) and the processes belonging to these objects, such as service charge settlement, option rate determination, third-party management, room reservation, and continuous occupancy. These features are only available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA.​

SAP strategy regarding use of SAP RE-FX for commercial and corporate real estate management:​

For commercial and corporate real estate business processes, enhancements and new feature development will be focused on the described “way forward” above.​

This means that although SAP RE-FX functions specific to real estate are only available in accordance with SAP Note 2254013, all future innovations (for example, additional AI capabilities, Fiori user interface improvements, SAP Business Data Cloud data products) are planned to be developed for SAP Contract and Lease Management and the integration to Planon.​

SAP strategy regarding use of SAP RE-FX for residential industry in DE, AT, CH​

For this specific industry, customers are advised to use RE-FX with all existing functionalities in accordance with SAP Note 2254013.​

Lease Accounting​

SAP Contract and Lease Management continues to be the strategic SAP solution for processing leasing requirements (IFRS 16, US GAAP 842, etc.) for all types of leased assets; this solution remains a focus of development efforts. ​

​

*************Disclaimer*************​

The information in this blog is confidential and proprietary to SAP and may not be disclosed without the permission of SAP. Except for your obligation to protect confidential information, this blog is not subject to your license agreement or any other service or subscription agreement with SAP. SAP has no obligation to pursue any course of business outlined in this blog or any related document, or to develop or release any functionality mentioned therein.​

This blog is provided without a warranty of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. entry.title: IRPA - count unique values in array
entry.content:
Hi, I am using activity get values (cells). This gives list of values. Using this variable and embedding it with ".length" it gives number of values. But how to count unique values? IRPA suggest things like ".count" or ".unique" but it gives empty result. I have also tried to use custom script like this one:

const arr = RunID;
function countUniqueArray(arr) {
const Result = [];
arr.forEach(item => {
if (!Result.includes(item)) {
Result.push(item);
}
});
return Result.length;
}

return(countUniqueArray(arr));

But issue is:

1) when RunID (returned values from excel) are passed directly, counting of unique values does not work and gives only total number of items correctly, but not unique values

2) when insted of RunID I hard code something like ["apple", "banana", "apple", "orange"] it works correctly and returns 3

So either change input to something like in 2) (but how) or use some javascript function directly in one of the steps.

Many thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP API OData Security Part III – OAuth2.0 Authorization Code Flow
entry.content:
Hi Ramin,

I doubt if its possible as it defeats the purpose of OAuth. OAuth by definition needs an account to be present in Service Provider application.

In real world application say xyz if you notice - whenever you authenticate xyz application via google or any other OAuth provider, first xyz application grabs neccessary details from google like email id, name or any other attribute and then xyz application creates such identity in its DB and then it lets you authenticate. Ultimately user must exist in xyz application before a successfull authentication.

const oGrid = await oView.Grid("UDO_GRID_@UDO1");

Anyone knows how can I do that properly?
 
Thank you very much!

entry.title: Data Purging Process in BW4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to purge the data in all adso's with retention period of 15 yrs.

For ADSO with time char, came across below option to design a solution for purging the data.

Option:

  1. Create a config table with ADSO names, Time char and org units. Creating an org table to maintain its values( If available, Cocode, Plant)
  2. Write an ABAP or AMDP program considering the above two tables data and system date for  deriving  period.
  3. Maintaining a log table to track deletion of ADSO data.
  4. Calling the program in Process chain to execute as a yearly activity.

Note: Stored procedure option is not preferred in our case as all jobs are scheduled in Process chains.

Could you please assist or help if above option is ok or is there any other better option available for better and efficient way. we have around 200 ADSOs to purge now.

Thanks,

Shyamala.

entry.title: Re: No cache region displays in SAP cloud commerce
entry.content:
Just set the property below to true

regioncache.stats.enabled=true

entry.title: Re: Error in Transformation Flow when using a remote table.
entry.content:
Found a solution in making the remote table a local table with a dataflow and then sharing the table to other spaces.

The specified table was one that has not yet a delta-cds-enabled on it, so bottom line, finally it would go anyway into a local table.

So problem solved.

entry.title: Automate User Assignment in Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Introduction

Digital Manufacturing (DM) plays a pivotal role for various personas on the shopfloor. During the implementation phase, primary users typically include Power Users like Manufacturing Admins and Production Engineers. However, as we prepare for the go-live stage, onboarding Production Operators and Production Supervisors becomes crucial.

This onboarding process involves several essential steps: setting up system access, assigning appropriate authorizations, and ensuring organizational alignment with plants and work centers.

In my previous blog User Onboarding, I provided an overview of these steps. In this post, I'll share a sample application demonstrating how you can use Public APIs provided by Digital Manufacturing to automate user assignment to plants and work centers.

Background

I am not a professional developer. I created this sample application for two purposes:

  1. To provide an example implementation for automation.
  2. To challenge myself and learn how AI tools can support me along the way.

The result? A functional sample application, now available on GitHub https://github.com/SAP-samples/digital-manufacturing-extension-samples/tree/main/dm-sidebyside-extension/SideBySide_userAssignmentApp 😊.

Regarding AI support, I was amazed at how well it worked! However, some basic knowledge is necessary to ask the right questions and interpret responses accurately. And when things get tricky or errors arise, having a knowledgeable colleague like Vivekananda Panigrahy can make all the difference. Thanks a lot, Vivek!

User Story

As an administrator, I want to:

  • Easily assign multiple production operators to plants and work centers.
  • Define template users with predefined certifications and work centers that can be used to create production operators.
  • Import initial user data via a CSV file.

Sample Solution

Master Data - Template user

In this sample I have created two users which I use to define certain user groups which can than be used as template to create the actual users.

  • UG_OP_PRODUCE
  • UG_OP_PACKING

The difference between them lies in their assigned Work Centers and a dummy certification, which I have assigned using the user ID. This certification allows me to easily identify the relationship between the created users and the user group used for their creation. 

Additionally, I created a UG_ALL user, which includes all user groups as supervised users. This makes it easy to retrieve all user groups defined in a plant.

 

Custom App

To build the application I used the approach of a side-by-side extension using SAP UI5 and public User APIs  provided by SAP DM https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_user/resource/User

The application consists of three main steps.

  • Upload user data
  • Retrieve and select a user group
  • Creation of the user

Step 1: Upload user

You can browse for the CSV file containing the users and upload it.

I followed the implementation logic outlined in this blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/creation-of-dynamic-table-with-csv-xlsx-files-using-file-upload-in-sap-ui5/ba-p/13478701, though I simplified it to focus on CSV format only.

The CSV file is very basic and only includes Email, First Name and Last Name.

A sample CSV file is provided with the sample code under the resources folder.

Step2: Retrieve and select the user group

In this step, the user group that should serve as a template is retrieved and selected. First the user needs to to enter:

  • The plant name.
  • The UG_ALL user (which contains all user groups).

 All the user groups assigned are listed and once one user group is selected from the result list the details of the user group is displayed to allow reviewing the details.

To retrieve the user groups, I call the Public API https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_user/path/getSupervisorsUsingGET_1 with the user UG_ALL.

The API returns the list of supervised users, which in this setup represents the different user groups available within a plant. To display the details I call the Public API https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_user/path/getUserUsingGET_1.

Step 3: Create users

Once the user data has been uploaded and the template user group selected, the creation of new users—including certification and work center assignments—can be triggered.

For user creation, I call API https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_user/path/createUserUsingPOST_1

Note: If a user already exists in DM, the creation process will fail due to an error. To handle this, you can modify the program logic to allow either creation or update based on user input.

For updating users, use: https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_user/path/updateUserUsingPATCH_1.

Reporting in DM – Embedded SAC

Why did I assign a certification named after the user group to each user? This trick allows me to easily retrieve users assigned to specific groups using the User Certification Assignment Master Data Object  and display it in a custom dashboard 
Details on the MODs you can find on SAP HELP - https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/insights/usercertification-mdo.

 

Sample code on GitHub

The sample code is published on GitHub SAP-samples/digital-manufacturing-extension-samples.
If you want to use it as a jump start make sure you read the Readme File to adapt the mandatory configurations.

1. Set Up a Destination in Your Subaccount

To call a public API in your coding you first need to setup a destination in your subaccount pointing to the Public API. Details you can find on SAP HELP https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/operations-guide/prepare-for-api-integration

2. Configure the SAP Cloud Application Router (xs-app.json)

In the xs-app.json file the configuration for your SAP Cloud Application Router, which helps route incoming requests within your app, is defined. Make sure it points to the destination you have defined.

3. Adjust environment configuration settings in SAP BTP

Issue: Many modern browsers, including Google Chrome, have tightened restrictions on third-party cookies to improve privacy and security. This can impact authentication flows or application behavior that relies on cookies for session management Solution: Navigate to the SAP BTP Space where your application is deployed. Locate your environment configuration settings. Add or modify the variable: COOKIE_BACKWARD_COMPATIBILITY=true This instructs SAP BTP to maintain compatibility with older cookie behavior, helping resolve login and session issues.

For more details I suggest investigating on the internet as I am not the expert.

Conclusion

The current version of Digital Manufacturing and the available Public APIs for Users allows you to easily automate the organizational assignment of users to work centers.

This sample application on GitHub serves as a jump-start for creating custom-tailored solutions, whether for integration with third-party tools, adding approval workflows, or refining user assignment strategies.

If you try this approach, let me know your experiences!

entry.title: Re: Unable to Post Goods Receipt for a Advanced Intercomp...
entry.content:
Check the Value Chain Monitoring app and make sure that the transfer of control was already posted from sending company code to receiving company code.

"transfer of control date" is a field in the outbound delivery header (tab shipment).

Only when the goods have been transferred to the receiving company code, you can post goods receipt in the plant.

In addition, check if the posting periods are open (transaction MMPV).

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning Hub Enablement Sessions - New webinars starting 29 May 2025
entry.content:
Hi @grant_boreham ,

Thank you for the opportunity to share suggestions for future webinar topics.

Coming from an academic background where structured mentoring played a key role in my growth, I have truly seen how impactful it can be—both in building confidence and navigating complex ecosystems. When I transitioned into SAP, I noticed that such mentoring activities were not as visible or accessible, especially given the vast scope of SAP's departments, modules, and learning paths.

I believe a session on "Mentoring in the SAP Ecosystem" could be highly beneficial. It could explore topics like:

  • How to find or become a mentor within the SAP Learning community
  • Building peer support networks across different SAP roles and modules
  • Sharing success stories from mentees/mentors
  • Tips for navigating SAP career paths through guided mentoring

This could especially help new professionals and career changers feel more connected and supported as they grow within the SAP landscape.

Thank you again for organizing this valuable series, and I hope this idea resonates with the goals of SAP Learning.

Best regards,

Folake

entry.title: Re: How to include ‘Data Action’ step if user click the ‘...

entry.title: Re: how to trigger staple functionality in local printer...
entry.content:
Dear Ganeshmasne,

When printing via S/4HANA Public Cloud no printer specific settings (like stapling or duplex) can be used by default. The reason for this is that in cloud-printing the printer is not known to the S/4HANA system and both operate in different networks. To bridge from the S/4HANA system to the printer, a print client (default SAP Cloud Print Manager - CPM) has to be used. The physical printer is hence only known to the print client. Partners might offer print clients with more capabilities. 

Regards,

Markus

entry.title: Re: Can the service bundle be pre-configured with specifi...
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, in the Service scope 3D2 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, it is possible to predefine the materials for service bundles. This ensures that users do not need to manually create material entries each time a service bundle is selected. Service bundles consist of a main item and one or more subitems, which can include service products, service parts, and expense items. Service - Service Bundles The service bundle can be configured to automatically include the predefined materials as part of the subitems linked to the main bundle item. This setup not only saves time but also standardizes the offerings provided to customers and aids in quick service processing. More detailed configuration and handling of service bundles can be found in the specific documentation on service bundles.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Populate default value in custom field added in Servi...
entry.content:
Hello,

To default a custom field value in the service order header and ensure it's populated during editing, you need to modify your implementation of the CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY BAdI. Here's how to address the issue:

  1. Correct the BAdI implementation:

    • Remove the statement serviceheader_extension_out = serviceheader_extension_in from your BAdI implementation .
    • This statement overwrites your new values with the old ones, preventing your custom logic from taking effect.

  2. Implement the correct logic:

    • In the BAdI method, assign your desired default value directly to the corresponding field in the serviceheader_extension_out structure .

  3. Ensure the BAdI is called during editing:

    • The BAdI should be triggered both when creating and editing service orders.
    • Double-check your BAdI implementation to ensure it's properly registered and activated.

  4. Consider using the SERVICE_TRANSACTION_CHECK BAdI:

    • If the CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY BAdI is still not triggered during editing, you may need to use the SERVICE_TRANSACTION_CHECK BAdI.
    • This BAdI allows you to check and modify service order data before saving.

  5. Verify custom field properties:

    • Ensure that the custom field properties are set correctly in the BAdI implementation.
    • The CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY BAdI is specifically designed to set field properties for extension fields at the solution order header level.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Compounded key value in AFO BOBJ report output
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
We use BOBJ reports based on AFO(Analysis for Office) and these reports are based on CDS views in SAP. We have recently upgraded our S4 system to 2023 version and post upgrade, we started seeing compounded values for some of the fields in the BOBJ output.
In the output of the report, we use formula SAPLISTOFEFFECTIVEFILTERS to display the filters that are used to run the query and in this, post upgrade we are getting compounded values(Combination of all key fields in corresponding table) instead of single values(Specific field value).
Have you come across this situation. If yes, can you please guide how to avoid this and show single values.
In below screenshot, Main transaction should show 0040, instead it is compounding with another key field from the table Application area which always has value 'R'.

Thank you,

Amarendra.

entry.title: Enhancing Data Integration and Collaboration through Asset Administration Shell in SAP BNAC
entry.content:
What is SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC)?

 SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC) solution offers a common foundation for collaborative asset management across different stakeholders involved in the lifecycle of physical assets—such as machinery, equipment, or infrastructure. By utilizing digital twins of these assets, SAP BNAC enhances collaboration among component suppliers, OEMs, and asset operators. This improves resilience, transparency, and operational reliability throughout the entire asset lifecycle. It also extends SAP S/4HANA for B2B integration of asset data and maintenance, using features from SAP Intelligent Asset Management and SAP Fieldglass solutions.

More information about SAP BNAC can be found here.

 

What are Digital Twins and why we need standards?

Simply speaking, digital twins can be seem as virtual representations of physical assets, systems, or processes. By mimicking real characteristics and behaviors of its physical counterparts during its lifecycle, digital twins provide numerous opportunities for business to provide insights into performance, maintenance needs, and enhance overall efficiency.

To fully exploit the potential of digital twins, data is collected from a variety of sensors and data sources with its own formats and data models. While this process partly occurs within the boundaries of a single company, it often involves numerous stakeholders throughout the product lifecycle. In this context, standards are crucial for digital twins to ensure consistency, interoperability, and seamless integration across different platforms and industries. This is where Asset Administration Shell (AAS) becomes significant.

 

Introducing Asset Administration Shell (AAS)

The Asset Administration Shell (AAS) is an industry standard (IEC 63278) for digital twins and driven by industrial leaders under the Industrial Digital Twin Association (IDTA). It provides standardized meta model and APIs allowing both unambiguous identification, representation of physical assets as well as interoperable integration to systems (data sources) relevant for digital twins. One of the features of the AAS is the concept of Submodels. Submodels organizes digital twin data following a specific use case or domain. These Submodels can adhere to established standards such as ECLASS and VDI 2770, ensuring interoperability across different systems, allowing for the seamless sharing and interaction of digital twin data. Well known examples of Submodels are Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation.

Example of an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) of a robot with Submodels.

  Figure 1 Example of an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) of a robot with Submodels.

 

Asset Administration Shell (AAS) support in SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC)

By providing support to the AAS, SAP BNAC establishes an interoperability foundation for component suppliers, OEMs, and operators, facilitating the use of digital twins in collaboration processes. The new feature will enable users to utilize AAS to create corresponding business objects in SAP BNAC and integrate them into business processes via SAP S/4HANA.

High-level overview of collaborative asset management leveraging AAS.

 Figure 2 High-level overview of collaborative asset management leveraging AAS.

  • Seamless and Interoperable Data Import:
    SAP BNAC now supports the effortless import of AAS files (AASX) through its user interface. This allows for the integration of Submodels like the Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation to create Equipment objects. This standardization simplifies data handling and ensures consistency.
  • Integration with SAP S/4HANA:
    The AAS import functionality translates AAS data into standard SAP objects, integrating them into SAP Plant Maintenance and SAP Material Master. This integration ensures that asset data is not only accurate but also easily accessible across the SAP ecosystem.
  • Extended Partner Collaboration:
    SAP BNAC extends its collaborative capabilities beyond the SAP ecosystem. It ensures interoperability with data ecosystems like Factory-X, enabling businesses to engage with a broader network of partners and service providers.

Figure 2 shows the use case leveraging AAS for asset management with SAP BNAC. In this use case, AAS can be used to transport data of digital twins from other data sources (e.g., engineering system, manufacturing system, etc.) into SAP BNAC. Alternatively, the user could also obtain the AAS from a supplier. With the new feature, SAP BNAC creates the corresponding business object by importing the AAS and allows their integration into SAP S/4HANA. Once the business objects are created, the user can share them with their business partners using SAP BNAC.

A robot manufacturer provides a digital twin of their products to their customers (operators). Typically, the manufacturer spends considerable time searching for component data from suppliers and manually maintaining this information in their SAP system. With SAP BNAC's new functionality, manufacturers can automatically create an Equipment with corresponding Documents by importing an AAS Instance that includes Submodels such as Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation. The manufacturer can then use existing SAP BNAC features to share the Equipment and Documents with their customer (operator). The operator can integrate the received Equipment and Documents into their SAP S/4HANA system, facilitating additional business processes like maintenance planning.

 

Begin Your Journey in Standardized Digital Twins with the SAP BNAC 2504 Release

Starting with 2504 release, SAP BNAC will enable the import of an AAS file (AASX) containing an AAS Instance with Submodel Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation through the user interface to create Equipment objects and the corresponding Documents.

Learn more about this feature: https://url.sap/x7hntw

Below are some of screenshots showing the new release. With the feature “Data Import” (Figure 4-7), users can now upload an AASX file (as shown Figure 3). Figure 8-10 shows the resulting Equipment and its Document as derived from AAS.

Figure 3 AASX file with the AAS Instance of a Safety Valve from LESER as modeled with Eclipse AASX Package Explorer from IDTA.

Figure 4 SAP BNAC initial UI with Data Import Feature highlighted.

Figure 5 AASX Import - Part 1: User click on "New" to import AAX File.

Figure 6 AASX Import - Part 2: Users select corresponding AASX file from AAS Instance of LESER Safety Valve as shown above.

Figure 7 AASX Import - Part 3: SAP BNAC processes the AASX file and generates the corresponding Equipment, Attributes and Documents accordingly.

Figure 8 AASX Import – Part 4: Resulting Equipment as derived from AASX file.

Figure 9 AASX Import - Part 5: Equipment Data Sheet containing the Attributes as derived form AAS Submodel Digital Nameplate.

Figure 10 AASX Import - Part 6: Documents derived from AAS Submodel Handover Documentation.

 

Conclusion

The 2504 release of SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC) marks a significant milestone in the realm of collaborative asset management. By supporting Asset Administration Shell (AAS), SAP BNAC offers an effortless and standardized way to import, integrate, and manage asset data as digital twins. This enhancement ensures that businesses can capitalize on the full potential of digital twins, fostering a more collaborative and efficient environment. Here is an overview of the value proposition:

  • Automatic Asset Data Creation:  Saves time and money by avoiding manual errors.
  • Easy Data Sharing: Allows manufacturers, operators, and service providers to focus on their business processes.
  • Integrate industry standards into your business processes: Synchronize digital twins with SAP S/4HANA (Plant Maintenance and Material Master).
  • Future-proof: Getting ready for Digital Product Passport (DPP) and collaborating in Factory-X.

 

Join us and be part of the Digital Twin Ecosystem

We are pleased to introduce this innovative feature and invite you to assist us in refining and perfecting it. Your feedback is invaluable as we enhance the AAS support in SAP BNAC. Join us in driving the future of digital twin technology.

Please contact us today (email ) to learn how SAP BNAC can transform your business operations.

At SAP, we are dedicated to more than just software development—we are pioneering the next wave of industrial innovation. Harness the power of digital twins to elevate your business to new heights.

This is only the beginning—additional enhancements are forthcoming as we continue to broaden our support for industry standards and cross-system connectivity. Stay tuned!

Further information:

  • SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration
  • SAP Help Portal - What's New in SAP BNAC
  • SAP Help Porta - Data Import (AAS Import)

entry.title: Project Kernseife - improving the measurement of Clean Core in ABAP
entry.content:
TL;DR

To improve the handling and measuring of classic ABAP code in ATC with regards to clean core, the possibility of an ATC check based on a custom classification was developed and is now published as an open source project named "Project Kernseife".

Challenges in Measuring Clean Core

Working in the area of clean core - especially focusing on the dimension "extensibility" - in projects of different scales, we were more than once asked in the last years on how to measure and govern the clean core principles within SAP S/4HANA systems (focusing on Private Cloud & On-Premises).

A key part to describe the "clean core state" of extensions is from our point of view the creation of a technical debt score that should align with the governance process in your system. That is why SAP's own code quality tool ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) is very helpful on this. Since the release of "ABAP Cloud" as target for On-Stack extensions, we have now possibibilities with the related ATC Check Variant (ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS) to identify the "distance to Tier 1".

But looking at landscapes with large amount of legacy code (e.g. > 100,000 custom code objects in classic ABAP) or systems where you cannot use sufficiently released APIs for Tier 1, it becomes more difficult to identify the "real" technical debt.

If you run with the default ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS variant into larger systems, you might be overwhelmed by a huge number of findings in ATC - that are all marked as Error. This is logical, if you want to identify the criticality as "what do I need to change to get into ABAP Cloud", but at the same time the important technical debt might be hidden behind a lot of "false positive" findings.

To improve this situation, SAP released the new 3 Tier check (SAP Note 3565942) in February of 2025. This check improves the situation by analyzing "classic" coding with a stronger focus on technical debt, to identify the "right" things. It is based on SAP's released objects (Tier 1, official APIs) and nominated Classic APIs. These Classic APIs belong either to business logic (e.g. BAPI_PO_CREATE1) or a technical framework (e.g. ALV). Finally, there are now also "noAPIs" (objects that must not be used, e.g. RFC_READ_TABLE) nominated by SAP.

Still - we saw a gap in projects to improve the lifes of lead developers: A custom classification. Because SAP may not be able to classify a certain ABAP object as precise as you may be able to do. So SAP is not publishing any information on a given ABAP object and it is shown as accessing an internal object (which leads in ATC to a warning). But in your use case, you are fine with using it. And this is where Kernseife can help you.

What is "Kernseife"?

Kernseife consists of two parts:

  1. A custom ATC Check that is based on the new Tier 3 check mentioned above, but uses a custom classification system.
  2. CAP Application to create a custom classification of ABAP objects (and we even use it to analyze results from systems in a simple way).

ATC Check for custom classification

As mentioned, we started to work on "Kernseife" in 2024 as there was no detailed Tier 3 check available then. Therefore, our focus was initially to create a custom ATC check that can work with a custom classification. It is now re-worked and based on the "new" ATC Check (Note 3565942).

This check is basically analyzing your custom code like the Tier 3 check, but when it comes to the evaluation of the impact of a finding, the custom classification comes into play. You can upload the custom classification as a JSON file and in this classification ywill then used to determine your findings. Let's explain the difference with an example:

In your project, you need to work with the RF framework, because you are implementing EWM in your Private Cloud Environment. Therefore, you need to work with the classes /SCWM/CL_RF*, that are the recommended way to interact with the RF framework. If you look into the Cloudifcation Repostory, you can see that these classes are not released or nominated as "classic API". Still, you want to generally accept the usage of the RF framework for both: Governance of developers (ATC Checks) and measuring your "distance to clean core".

By using your custom classification which contains the classes /SCWM/CL_RF* as "Business Function - Recommended" (BF1), which will lead to only "Information" (Prio 3) findings in the check and will have a lower impact on the "clean core score", you can improve your efficiency.

For details on the custom classification, you can check the classification guide.

Application to manage Classifications

Of course, you could maintain the JSON file of the classification manually based on the given json format. But we created a little app, that is currently in a feature branch, to do that in a more sophisticated way.

In this application, you can initially check the current state for objects in as defined by SAP and additionally add objects that or not yet mentioned by SAP.

Sticking with the example from above, you can now edit the "Rating Code" of the /SCWM/CL_RF* classes to "BF1". After you did your changes, you can download the classification JSON file, upload it into your ATC check system and all of the findings for these classes will in future be recorded as "Information" (Prio 3) message.

Governance and Measurability

With this custom classification and the usage in ABAP Test Cockpit, we can now combine the governance view and the measurability within ATC.

Governance

By enhancing SAP's default rating, you can control your developers more efficiently. Because as of today, your lead developers that are able to approve exemptions might be overwhelmed by a lot of "false positive" ATC findings. Just imagine every usage of the example classes "/SCWM/CL_RF*" need to be exempted.

In projects - especially looking at the overwhelming errors from the "ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS" ATC check in classic ABAP, you can now control with your custom classification, that recurring "false positive" findings will disappear (or become prio 3 messages only).

This is important, as - being a reviewer - your chances to ask questions on the important findings will improve. Because if for every major developments you need to approve hundreds of exemption requests, you will most probably go into "autopilot" approval mode as you cannot check all of the requests in detail.

Measurability

Our second motivation to create this check is to create a more realistic impact rating to measure what we call the "Kernseife Score" (basically: a technical debt score per object). With every classification (BF1), we consider a score of how severe the impact of using this SAP object is with regards to clean core.

So you can configure now a score - we did use a 0 to 10 scale - to identify how severe is a single finding. Based on the ATC check, we can now provide per object a score that you can use for your reporting. Either to identify the total score per system, per package, per object - or even to deep dive into analyses like "which object has the worst average score per finding?" or "which object is in total view the "uncleanest" object in my system?".

While you can then easily use your analytics tools (e.g. SAP Analytics Cloud) to create the dashboards you need, in our repository you can at least find a CDS-View you can use for extraction (ZKNSF_I_SCORING), that combines the findings with the score. If you want to directly extract e.g. a list aggregated by object, you could directly use another CDS-View (ZKNSF_I_DEVELOPMENT_OBJECTS).

What's next?

Kernseife is currently in use (or in prior versions) in several projects. But to be very clear: There is development ongoing, but we do not have a product roadmap available.

But as it is open source, we are open to feature discussions or pull requests - or just use it for your own advantage!

entry.title: Sending multiple sales / billing documents in one e-mail
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there any way to consolidate multiple Order confirmations or Billing documents into one e-mail?

We could have multiple sales order created for the same customer the same day and the customer would appreciate only getting one e-mail with all order confirmations instead of being bombarded with e-mails with order confirmations. 

Same thing with Billing document. We invoice the same customer multiple orders the same day. We need to have separate invoices. But the customer would appreciate to get one e-mail with all invoices attachements instead of 100 separate e-mails.

Kind Regards,

Cecilia

entry.title: Re: Service Quotations (Requested Start Date - End Date)
entry.content:
Hello,

Reasons for Date Adjustments

End Date Change

The automatic adjustment of the end date could be linked to system configurations or custom logic implemented via SAP’s framework. For instance, Configurable Parameter and Formula (CPF) framework allows Flexible Proposal of Requested Delivery Dates on Sales Order for dynamic adjustments based on rules defined by your system administrators source.

Posting Date Delay

The discrepancy in the posting date can arise due to standard system behavior using system dates Sales Order change/create times are unclear instead of user-entered dates. This might be influenced by system time settings, reflecting transactions based on server dates rather than those set by users source.

These adjustments are generally in place to accommodate business processes and ensure operational consistency within workflows.

The service may have lead time, the lead time is considered and may affect your end date.

Recommendations

  • Check Configuration: Review any custom settings or rules associated with service quotation dates to ensure they are set as intended.
  • Consult Documentation: If CPF rules might be affecting date results, consulting documentation or your system administrator for changes could be a solution.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

I've created a simple RAP Fiori report on BW4Hana side. 

What My goal would be is to launch process chains based on the values of the row selected.

Problem is I've stucked on the 0. th step. 


I've created the action button in the metadata extension and implemented the launchProcess method to just launch one specific processchain only as a first step.

 

 

CLASS lhc_ZS_DD_PLANNING_EV DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.

PRIVATE SECTION.

 

METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION

METHOD launchProcess.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG Parallel processing in Mass processing of data
entry.content:
Hi

The Consolidation and Mass Processing has been build to handle data at object level and on activation of the request, it is looking to lock the entire object into the CR. Hence any Change Request Type which has scope limitation which would be done in case of parallel processing will not show up. 

A Classic indicator for this is that when we load one plant of a material, the Mass Processing is going to pull the entire data and process it.....so when writing back it would also do the same.

You need to relook at the design to consider this and have an alternate solution.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Sending multiple sales / billing documents in one e-mail
entry.content:
Hello,

The scenario described involves consolidating multiple order confirmations or billing documents into a single email. SAP S/4HANA provides functionality that can help in similar scenarios, although specific configurations for email consolidation may need to be manually implemented or custom configured, as there’s no direct built-in setting mentioned in the available documents.

Consolidating Order Confirmations

For order confirmations, SAP’s communication arrangement for email notifications can be configured. Using tools like Output Parameter Determination, you can generate email outputs based on grouping criteria. However, it primarily focuses on sending individual outputs per document unless specifically configured otherwise.

Consolidating Billing Documents

Convergent Billing and Invoice Lists

SAP S/4HANA offers omnichannel convergent billing Sales Billing where you could converge billing data from multiple sources into a combined invoice under specific conditions. This convergent billing can help streamline customer communications.

Solution Billing

Solution Billing allows consolidating billing data Solution Billing from multiple products and services into one combined invoice under solution orders, though this may not directly address separate invoices sent together.

Practical Steps

  • Output Parameter Determination: Use the Output Parameter Determination app How to Configure Output Parameter Determination to define business rules that consolidate the output formats or send multiple attachments in a single email.
  • Custom Implementation: You might need to implement custom code to aggregate and send attachments altogether.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Add Application Autoscaler Service Binding To Deployed Application For High Concurrency Situation
entry.content:
Background Introduction

In some situation, customers find existing application can meet their requirements functionally. But customers have concerns about the performance. For example, we have build mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js, but customers have concerns for high concurrency situation. Then does customer need to adjust the existing code? The answer is no need. Customer can add Application Autoscaler Service Binding To Deployed Application and create Scaling Policy for the deployed application.

Steps:

Step 1. Add Application Autoscaler Service Binding To Deployed Backend Application

The following Json file defines the Scaling Policy. I will explain it a little later .

{
  "instance_min_count": 1,
  "instance_max_count": 5,
  "scaling_rules": [
    {
      "metric_type": "memoryused",
      "breach_duration_secs": 600,
      "threshold": 90,
      "operator": ">=",
      "cool_down_secs": 300,
      "adjustment": "+1"
    },
    {
      "metric_type": "memoryused",
      "breach_duration_secs": 600,
      "threshold": 30,
      "operator": "<",
      "cool_down_secs": 300,
      "adjustment": "-1"
    }
  ]
}

Step 2. Restart your backend application.

Step 3. You can check your scaler history in Application Autoscaler Dashboard.

Explanation for Scaling Policy

You can define a policy to scale your application instances either dynamically or based on schedule.

If you want to scale your application instances based on memory or CPU usage, response time, throughput, or custom metrics. You can use Dynamic Scaling Policy

If you want scale your application instances based on schedules, you can use Schedule-Based Scaling Policy.

 

The Ends!

Thanks for your time!

Jacky Liu

entry.title: Re: How to tackle code adaptations for S/4HANA for IS-Hea...
entry.content:
Hi @BaerbelWinkler ,

My solution was as follows:

- if possible find a standard replacement, either for FM or message classes
- if not possible, then create Z-objects for it.

For message classes I was always searching the for the text, and I think I always found a replacement.
For missing FM/classes it was more tricky sometimes, as sometimes it's not simple class or FM to display something, but with the calculations. For most of them I found easily a replacement for the missing FM. Where we haven't found it, we have started deeper checks, what has changed that the FM is missing. In we cases we just omitted the part or we had to adapt our logic in program to follow new process/new way of SAP. 
But wherever possible I reused the exisitng objects. 

I am sure there are plenty of FM and classes to display log and to show the popup to confirm. FORMAT_DATE_4_OUTPUT I don't know, but date formating can be also done in string expression.

 

Cheers
Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Search for Specific Item on VA03
Re: Internal Order Budgeting
entry.content: In S/4HANA, SAP automatically fetches the technical clearing account during the Purchase Requisition (PR) stage. In contrast, in ECC, the Asset Acquisition GL account is fetched. As a result, in S/4HANA, the effect is neutralized, and no budget is consumed at the end.

entry.title: SFC-Based Dispatching in REO: Granular Production Scheduling and Process Industry Support
entry.content:
Introduction

As manufacturers look for agility and responsiveness, the ability to adapt production schedules in real-time becomes critical.

SAP Digital Manufacturing’s 'SFC-Based Dispatching' capability in Resource Orchestration (REO) is a step forward in enabling manufacturers to dynamically control production at the most granular level, the Shop Floor Control (SFC) unit.

It is also the first step on enabling proper Process Industries support in REO (MVP, Limited Release Feature), and handling its complexities, see details further down below.

What is SFC-Based Dispatching?

So far, Resource Orchestration (REO) scheduling has been done at the operation/phase level, where individual operations or phases are dispatched to Resources or Work Centers (Production Lines). While effective, this less granular approach can have limitations—especially when considering resource capacities, handling exceptions, or responding to urgent production changes.

SFC-Based Dispatching shifts the focus from scheduling operations or phases to scheduling more granular production units: SFCs or Charges. This enables more refined scheduling and resource allocation, enhancing overall operational flexibility.

In simple terms, this means each part or batch in an order gets its own path through the factory. Instead of treating 100 'quantities' as one large batch for a given operation, the system can create separate schedules for each SFC or Charge and dispatch each one to the appropriate resource or work center at the right time.

See a visual example below:

Business Benefits

Here are some of the main businesses benefits:

Enhanced Production Flexibility

With SFC-level scheduling, you can dispatch specific units based on real-time conditions, such as resource capacity, material availability, machine uptime, or priority shifts. This is ideal for:

- Make-to-Order or highly customized products
- Frequent design or routing changes
- Dynamic environments with variable capacities and workloads

Improved Resource Utilization

By aligning individual SFCs with optimal resources, manufacturers can maximize throughput without overloading any specific work center.

Visibility and Control

In Dispatching and Monitoring app, planners and supervisors can see a clear differentiation between order-based and SFC-based schedules, helping teams focus on execution rather than coordination.

Tight Execution / POD Integration

Production Operator Dashboard (POD) execution is in sync with REO, as SFC scheduled can only show up and be executed in the planned resource, if the system configured that way.

How to Get Started

Enabling SFC-based scheduling requires minimal configuration, see SFC-Based Dispatching in Resource Orchestration for more details.

Process Industry Use Case

With SFC-Based Dispatching, Resource Orchestration (REO) support for Process Orders is officially introduced as a minimum viable product (MVP), and will keep evolving in the next couple of releases, so stay tuned in the next couple of releases!

 
Currently, this is how the flow looks like for Process Orders:
  • Partial Order Release

Once process orders are received from ERP, they can be partially released, creating individual SFCs (Charges) for specific batches with different quantities. 

  • SFC Schedules

Then, individual SFC/Charge schedules can be created, so these SFCs can then be scheduled independently to different resources.

  • SFC-Based Scheduling

Schedule individual SFCs (Charges) to Resources using existing scheduling capabilities and logic, which will ideally dispatch individual SFCs (Charges) to the optimum resources based on capacity, load and other constraints.

In Dispatching and Monitoring app, planners and supervisors can see a clear differentiation between order-based and SFC-based schedules, which are clearly marked by the green borders.

  • In Sync with Execution

Production Operator Dashboard (POD) execution is tightly integrated and in sync with REO, as SFC scheduled can only show up and be executed in the planned resource, if the system configured that way.

 
* See SFC-Based Dispatching in Resource Orchestration for more details.
* Keep in mind that Resource Orchestration (REO) support for Process Orders is introduced as a minimum viable product (MVP), so several restrictions apply.

Conclusion

SFC-Based Dispatching is not just a technical enhancement it’s a strategic tool. It allows manufacturers to shift from rigid planning cycles to agile execution, and is the very first step of enabling REO for Process Industries and its complexities. For production planners and supervisors, this feature offers a clear path to smarter, more adaptive manufacturing.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa
